title: brachycladium compositum
side_effects: Brachycladium compositum can have side effects if used improperly, such as diarrhea, stomach pain, and vomiting due to its toxic compounds like saponins and alkaloids. Ingestion of large amounts can lead to more severe symptoms like headaches, dizziness, and even seizures in extreme cases. Additionally, improper use of brachycladium compositum may also cause allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues in sensitive individuals. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy to avoid potential side effects.
